content.full_text:
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

February 1, 1999


Martha Clark Lofgren
City Attorney
City of Folsom
50 Natoma Street
Folsom, California 95630

Re: Your Request for Advice
    Our File No. I-98-305

Dear Ms. Lofgren:

     This letter is in response to your request for informal advice on behalf of the City of Folsom ("city") regarding the mass mailing provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

QUESTIONS

     1. May the city include biographical information about its council members in a newsletter if the information is prepared without consultation with the council members?

     2. May the city prepare the biographical information about its council members and allow the council members to review the information prior to including that information in the newsletter?

     3. If a council member reviews the biographical information prior to publication in a newsletter and requests revisions to the biographical information, will the information be considered to have been prepared or sent in cooperation, consultation, coordination, or concert with the elected officer?

     4. Will it matter if the city provides the biographical information about all of the council members in one issue of the newsletter or if it devotes separate articles to each council member in separate issues of the newsletter?


¹ Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109 - 18995, of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. I-98-305
Page 2

5. May the city include a photograph of a council member along with a story that provides the biographical information?

6. May the city include the name of a council member in the headline for the story in a type size that is larger than the type size used in the story containing the biographical information?

CONCLUSION

1. Generally, the city may not include any biographical information regarding a city councilmember if the newsletter is prepared or sent in cooperation, consultation, coordination, or concert with a city councilmember. In addition, biographical information may not be included in the city's newsletter if the newsletter "features" a city councilmember. See Analysis below.

2. No. If a city councilmember reviews the content of his or her biographical information, the newsletter would be prepared or sent in cooperation, consultation, coordination, or concert with the city councilmember.

3. If a city councilmember reviews the content and requests revision of his or her biographical information, the newsletter would be prepared or sent in cooperation, consultation, coordination, or concert with the city councilmember.

4. To the extent this may affect whether or not any particular city councilmember may be "featured" in particular issue of the newsletter, this fact may be relevant. If there is cooperation or consultation etc. with a city councilmember, the timing of the newsletter will not affect the fact that the prohibition applies.

5. No. Including a picture of a city councilmember would be "featuring" an elected officer affiliated with the agency and is prohibited regardless of whether or not the newsletter is prepared or sent in cooperation, consultation, coordination, or concert with the city councilmember.

6. No. That would be featuring the city councilmember and is prohibited.

FACTS

The City of Folsom (the "city") prepares a bimonthly newsletter concerning matters of interest to its citizens. The city distributes the newsletter at city expense through regular mail. Approximately 18,000 copies of the newsletter are mailed to residences located within the city. The costs of design, production, and mailing exceeds $50 for each issue of the newsletter.

File No. I-98-305
Page 3

The city has hired a consultant to produce the mailing. The consultant determines the
content of the mailing. The consultant's work is reviewed by the city manager. In some
instances, the consultant may contact a councilmember in connection with a particular story. It is
not the practice of city councilmembers to review the newsletter before it is sent to the public.

The city would like to include biographical information concerning its council members
in an upcoming edition of the newsletter. The purpose for providing this biographical
information would be to provide city residents with general information about their elected
officials, including the various committees and commissions on which the officials serve as part
of their official duties for the city.

ANALYSIS

Section 89001 states:

"No newsletter or other mass mailing shall be sent at public
expense."

Regulation 18901 (copy enclosed) interprets Section 89001:

"(a) Except as provided in subdivision (b), a mailing is
prohibited by section 89001 if all of the following criteria are met:
(1) Any item sent is delivered, by any means, to the recipient at
his or her residence, place of employment or business, or post
office box. For purposes of this subdivision (a)(1), the item
delivered to the recipient must be a tangible item, such as a
videotape, record, or button, or a written document.
(2) The item sent either:
(A) Features an elected officer affiliated with the agency which
produces or sends the mailing, or
(B) Includes the name, office, photograph, or other reference to
an elected officer affiliated with the agency which produces or
sends the mailing, and is prepared or sent in cooperation,
consultation, coordination, or concert with the elected officer.
(3)(A) Any of the costs of distribution is paid for with public
moneys; or
(B) Costs of design, production, and printing exceeding $50.00
are paid with public moneys, and the design, production, or
printing is done with the intent of sending the item other than as
permitted by this regulation.
(4) More than two hundred substantially similar items are sent, in
a single calendar month, excluding any item sent in response to an

File No. I-98-305
Page 4

unsolicited request and any item described in subdivision (b).
(b) [provides exceptions to the general rule.]"

A Newsletter that Includes Biographical Information of City Council Members

A newsletter which includes biographical information of the Folsom City Council clearly
meets the criteria of subdivisions (a)(1), (a)(3), and (a)(4) of Regulation 18901. First, the
newsletter would be a tangible item sent to people's residences. Second, the cost of the mailing
would be paid for with public funds and exceed $50.00. Third, more than 200 unsolicited copies
of the proposed mailing would be sent.

Whether subdivision (a)(2) is met is a more difficult question. That subdivision is met if
either (a)(2)(A) or (a)(2)(B) is met. First, we will discuss subdivision (a)(2)(A) and then
subdivision (a)(2)(B). Under subdivision (a)(2)(A), it is clear that individual city
councilmembers are affiliated with the city. (Regulation 18901(c)(1).) However, since we do
not have a copy of the newsletter you plan on sending out, we cannot conclusively determine
whether any city councilmember is "featured."

Regulation 18901 states that a mailing "'features an elected officer' if the item includes
the elected officer's photograph or signature, or singles out the elected officer by the manner of
display of his or her name or the office in the layout of the document, such as by headlines,
captions, type size, typeface, or type color." (Regulation 18901(c)(2).) Therefore, in response to
question #5 and #6, it is clear that under those circumstances the city councilmember would be
featured. However, absent a photograph or the inclusion of a city councilmember's name in a
headline, it is not clear, without the actual newsletter, whether any city councilmember would be
"featured." In response to question #4, whether only one city councilmember's biography is
included in a particular newsletter may affect whether a city councilmember is featured. If there
is cooperation or consultation etc. with a city councilmember as discussed below, the timing of a
newsletter will not affect the fact that the prohibition will apply. However, without actual
newsletters to review and/or compare and facts pertaining to the councilmember's involvement,
we cannot determine how this fact may change the analysis.

Subdivision (a)(2)(B) is satisfied if the mailing includes the name, office, photograph, or
any other reference to an elected officer affiliated with the agency if the mailing "is prepared or
sent in cooperation, consultation, coordination, or concert with the elected officer." (Regulation
18901(a)(2)(B).) A newsletter that includes biographical information of a city councilmember
will include the name of the city councilmember. Also, as stated above, a city councilmember is
affiliated with the city. (Regulation 18901(c)(1).) Therefore, we are left to determine if any
mailing in your questions "is prepared or sent in cooperation, consultation, coordination, or
concert with the elected officer."

File No. I-98-305
Page 5

We have interpreted this language very broadly. Some excerpts from previous advice
follow:

•

"A mailing is prepared or sent in cooperation, consultation, coordination or concert
with an elected officer if the elected officers affiliated with an agency are in any way
involved with the production or distribution of the items sent. This means, for example,
that the elected officers affiliated with the agency may not review the mailing or make
suggestions for articles to be included." (Culver Advice Letter, No. A-90-140 [Emphasis
added].)

•

"We believe that suggestions from board members as to the contents of the district's
newsletter result in the newsletter being prepared in cooperation, consultation,
coordination or concert with the board members." (Stone Advice Letter, No. I-89-670.)

•

"Review of the newsletter by the councilmember would be sufficient for the newsletter
to have been 'prepared or sent in cooperation, consultation, coordination, or concert' with
the councilmember." (Whelihan Advice Letter, No. I-89-063.)

Finally, in the Dunlap Advice Letter, I-90-311, we stated that, "Biographies and
photographs of affiliated elected officers may not be included in any publicly funded newsletter,
with the exception of any newsletter that falls within one of the exceptions set forth in the
regulation."

Based on the above, we must also conclude that any proposed mailing sent out by the city
that would include biographical information of city councilmembers would result in a violation
of Section 89001 unless an exception under subdivision (b) of Regulation 18901 is applicable to
your facts. However, because of the difficulty of the issue and the hypothetical nature of some of
the questions, we would be willing to reexamine this conclusion if you are willing to provide an
actual newsletter to review and if the city plans to prepare and send out a newsletter containing
biographical information of its councilmembers completely independent from the city
councilmembers.

Subsections (b)(1) and (b)(8) of Regulation 18901 provide relevant exceptions to the
mass mailing prohibition:

"(8) A telephone directory, organization chart, or similar listing
or roster which includes the names of elected officers as well as
other individuals in the agency sending the mailing, where the
name of each elected officer and individual listed appears in the
same type size, typeface, and type color. Such item may not
include an elected officer's photograph, name, signature, or any
other reference to an elected officer, except as specifically

File No. I-98-305
Page 6

permitted in this subdivision (b)(8) or elsewhere in this
regulation."

According to Regulation 18901(b)(8), you may list the names of the board with a
telephone number on how to reach them or include an organizational chart with the same
information, if you otherwise comply with Regulation 18901(b)(8). However, the listing of the
occupations of each board member or other biographical information is not allowed under
Regulation 18901(b)(8).

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at
(916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

[signature]

By: Marte Castaños
Staff Counsel, Legal Division

2. No. If a city councilmember reviews the content ofhis or her biographical
information, the newsletter would be prepared or sent in cooperation, consultation, coordination,
or concert with the city councilmember.
3. If a city councilmember reviews the content and requests revision ofhis or her

5. No. Including a picture of a city councilmember would be “featuring” an elected

sections.analysis:
Section 89001 states:
“No newsletter or other mass mailing shall be sent at public
expense.”
Regulation 18901 (copy enclosed) interprets Section 89001:
“(a) Except as provided in subdivision (b), a mailing is
prohibited by section 89001 if all of the following criteria are met:

his or her residence, place of employment or business, or post

sends the mailing, and is prepared or sent in cooperation,

moneys; or

are paid with public moneys, and the design, production, or
printing is done with the intent of sending the item other than as
permitted by this regulation.
(4) More than two hundred substantially similar items are sent, in
a single calendar month, excluding any item sent in response to an
unsolicited request and any item described in subdivision (b).
(b) [provides exceptions to the general rule.1.”
A Newsletter that Includes Biographical Information ofCity Council Members

meets the criteria of subdivisions (a)(1), (a)(3), and (a)(4) of Regulation 18901. First, the
newsletter would be a tangible item sent to people’s residences. Second, the cost of the mailing
would be paid for with public funds and exceed $50.00. Third, more than 200 unsolicited copies
of the proposed mailing would be sent.
Whether subdivision (a)(2) is met is a more difficult question. That subdivision is met if
either (a)(2)(A) or (a)(2)(B) is met. First, we will discuss subdivision (a)(2)(A) and then
subdivision (a)(2)(B). Under subdivision (a)(2)(A), it is clear that individual city
councilmembers are affiliated with the city. (Regulation 18901 (c)(1).) However, since we do
not have a copy ofthe newsletter you plan on sending out, we cannot conclusively determine
whether any city councilmember is “featured.”
Regulation 18901 states that a mailing “features an elected officer’ ifthe item includes
the elected officer’s photograph or signature, or singles out the elected officer by the manner of•
display ofhis or her name or the office in the layout of the document, such as by headlines,
captions, type size, typeface, or type color.” (Regulation 1890 1(c)(2).) Therefore, in response to
question #5 and #6, it is clear that under those circumstances the city councilmember would be
featured. However, absent a photograph or the inclusion of a city councilmember’s name in a
headline, it is not clear, without the actual newsletter, whether any city councilmember would be
“featured.” In response to question #4, whether only one city councilmember’s biography is

is cooperation or consultation etc. with a city councilmember as discussed below, the timing of a
newsletter will not affect the fact that the prohibition will apply. However, without actual
newsletters to review andlor compare and facts pertaining to the councilmember’s involvement,
we cannot determine how this fact may change the analysis.
Subdivision (a)(2)(B) is satisfied if the mailing includes the name, office, photograph, or
any other reference to an elected officer affiliated with the agency fthe mailing “is prepared or
sent in cooperation, consultation, coordination, or concert with the elected officer.” (Regulation
18901(a)(2)(B).) A newsletter that includes biographical information of acity councilmember
will include the name of the city councilmember.
Also, as stated above, a city councilmember is
affiliated with the city. (Regulation 18901 (c)(1).) Therefore, we are left to determine if any
mailing in your questions “is prepared or sent in cooperation, consultation, coordination, or
concert with the elected officer.”
We have interpreted this language very broadly. Some excerpts from previous advice
follow:
•

with an elected officer if the elected officers affiliated with an agency are in any way
involved with the production or distribution of the items sent. This means, for example,

suggestions for articles to be included.” (Culver Advice Letter, No. A-90-140 [Emphasis
added].)
•
“We believe that suggestions from board members as to the contents of the district’s
newsletter result in the newsletter being prepared in cooperation, consultation,
coordination or concert with the board members.” (Stone Advice Letter, No. 1-89-670.)
•
“Review of the newsletter by the councilmember would be sufficient for the newsletter
to have been ‘prepared or sent in cooperation, consultation, coordination, or concert’ with
the councilmember.” (Whelihan Advice Letter, No. 1-89-063.)
Finally, in the Dunlap Advice Letter, 1-90-311, we stated that, “Biographies and
photographs of affiliated elected officers may not be included in any publicly funded newsletter,
with the exception of any newsletter that falls within one of the exceptions set forth in the
regulation.”
Based on the above, we must also conclude that any proposed mailing sent out by the city

the questions, we would be willing to reexamine this conclusion if you are willing to provide an

biographical information of its councilmembers completely independent from the city
councilmembers.
Subsections (b)(1) and (b)(8) of Regulation 18901 provide relevant exceptions to the
mass mailing prohibition:
“(8) A telephone directory, organization chart, or similar listing
or roster which includes the names of elected officers as well as
other individuals in the agency sending the mailing, where the
name of each elected officer and individual listed appears in the
same type size, typeface, and type color. Such item may not
include an elected officer’s photograph, name, signature, or any
other reference to an elected officer, except as specifically
permitted in this subdivision (b)(8) or elsewhere in this
regulation.”
According to Regulation 18901(b)(8), you may list the names of the board with a

occupations of each board member or other biographical information is not allowed under
